description: Master label specification for STING seed tag families. Maps directly to Revit Edit Label dialog columns (Prefix, Suffix, Spaces, Break). The 'scheduled text words' — static natural language text that wraps around parameter values to produce corporate-standard presentation.

Compact.description: Basic identification — tag code + type name only

Technical.description: Engineering documentation — key properties + threshold warnings

Full_Specification.description: Complete specification — all tiers + paragraph + warnings

Presentation.description: Client presentation — clean tags without warning text

BOQ.description: Bill of Quantities — identity + quantities for cost schedules

cv_show_tier2.name: Show Tier 2
cv_show_tier2.description: Gates tier 2 parameters — visible when Standard or above

cv_show_tier3.name: Show Tier 3
cv_show_tier3.description: Gates tier 3 parameters — visible when Comprehensive

cv_show_warning.name: Show Warning
cv_show_warning.description: Gates warning text — visible when warnings enabled

parameter_text._comment: Global prefix/suffix definitions. These are the 'scheduled text words' for Edit Label.

ASS_TAG_2_TXT.label: Short Tag (FUNC-PROD-SEQ)
ASS_TAG_2_TXT.note: Heading line in TAG7 — underlined in State 2, bold+underlined in State 3. Style controlled via batch command.

parameter_text._paragraph_comment: TAG7 paragraph containers — tier_3 natural-language specification paragraphs. No prefix/suffix needed as content is a pre-assembled descriptive statement from the formula engine.

category_labels._comment: Per-category label definitions. tier_1 = always visible (Compact+), tier_2 = Standard+ (gated by TAG_PARA_STATE_2_BOOL), tier_3 = Comprehensive (gated by TAG_PARA_STATE_3_BOOL). Each entry maps directly to one row in Revit Edit Label. 'cv' prefix means use a calculated value for visibility gating.

Walls.paragraph_template: This {function} wall is {thickness} mm thick, constructed using {method} with {material} as the primary material. The wall is classified as {type} and achieves a thermal transmittance of {U-value} with a thermal resistance of {R-value}, assessed as {condensation} condensation risk. It provides {FRR} minutes of fire resistance with an acoustic rating of {STC} decibels. The assembly incorporates {recycled}% recycled content with an embodied carbon of {carbon} per unit. Internal finish is {int_finish} and external finish is {ext_finish}, with {moisture} moisture barrier provision. Compliant with {standard}.

tier_2.note: Short tag heading (FUNC-PROD-SEQ) — underlined in family

tier_3.note: Short tag heading (FUNC-PROD-SEQ) — bold underlined in family
Natural language paragraph — purely descriptive, no technical codes

Floors.paragraph_template: This floor slab is {thickness} mm thick with a {finish} finish, achieving a load capacity of {load} kilopascals. The assembly provides a thermal transmittance of {U-value} and {FRR} minutes of fire resistance. It incorporates {recycled}% recycled content with an embodied carbon of {carbon} per unit, meeting {standard} requirements.

tier_2.note: Short tag heading (FUNC-PROD-SEQ) — underlined in family

tier_3.note: Short tag heading (FUNC-PROD-SEQ) — bold underlined in family
Natural language paragraph — purely descriptive, no technical codes

Ceilings.paragraph_template: This ceiling is a {system} type installed at {height} mm above finished floor level on a {grid} mm suspension grid. The assembly provides {FRR} minutes of fire resistance and achieves a noise reduction coefficient of {NRC}. Finished in {finish}, compliant with {standard}.

Roofs.paragraph_template: This roof assembly is pitched at {slope} degrees with {covering} as the primary covering material. It achieves a thermal transmittance of {U-value} and provides {FRR} minutes of fire resistance. The structure features {drainage} drainage with {insulation} insulation, compliant with {standard}.

Doors.paragraph_template: This is a {width} by {height} millimetre {operation} door constructed from {material}. It provides {FRR} minutes of fire resistance and is fitted with {hardware} hardware. The frame is {frame_material} with {glazing} glazing where applicable, compliant with {standard}.

The window provides a solar heat gain coefficient of {SHGC} and a visible light transmittance of {VLT} percent, compliant with {standard}.

tag7_heading_style._comment: Controls the visual formatting of the TAG7 short tag heading (ASS_TAG_2_TXT) in both tier_2 and tier_3. These settings are applied at the tag family label level in Revit Family Editor.

tier_2_heading.note: Underlined in State 2 (Technical) — functional emphasis without visual dominance

tier_3_heading.note: Bold + Underlined in State 3 (Full Specification) — primary heading treatment

batch_control.description: Use the Set TAG7 Heading Style command to batch-update the heading format across all tag families. Supported styles: bold, italic, underline, bold+underline, bold+italic, all.
batch_control.applies_to: ASS_TAG_2_TXT label row in all STING tag families
